title: Coffee and walnut cake
subtitle: British two-layer coffee sponge with coffee buttercream
excerpt: The classic British coffee cake: two layers of coffee-flavoured sponge with chopped walnuts folded through the batter, sandwiched and covered with coffee buttercream. Decorated with walnut halves. The coffee flavour should be assertive, not background.
sourceNotes: Coffee cake with walnut and butter icing is documented in British domestic cooking from the late nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: the chapter on iced cakes documents a creamed butter sponge flavoured with coffee essence and finished with a coffee-flavoured butter icing, noting that the coffee should be strong enough to taste distinctly through the sweetness of the icing. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): the coffee and walnut layer cake is documented as a standard British afternoon tea cake, with walnuts in the sponge and the icing.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted sponge layers. Defrost at room temperature and frost before serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The frosted cake keeps at room temperature for 2 days, refrigerated for 4 days. Bring to room temperature for 30 minutes if refrigerated before serving.

glossaryTerms.definition: A buttercream flavoured with dissolved instant coffee or espresso. The coffee must be dissolved in a small amount of hot water before being beaten into the butter and icing sugar — adding dry granules produces uneven flavour pockets. Make the coffee solution stronger than you think you need: sweetness in the butter and icing sugar will mute the flavour, so the dissolved coffee should taste quite strong before it goes in.

content.text: Coffee and walnut cake is a straightforward layer cake that succeeds or fails on the coffee: if the flavour is timid, the cake is just a plain walnut sponge with a sweet topping. Use enough instant coffee that the sponge tastes distinctly of coffee before the buttercream goes on, and make the buttercream with the same intensity.

items.prepNote: dissolved in 2 tbsp hot water and cooled

content.text: Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Butter and line two round tins. Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} for the sponge with the {{caster-sugar}} until very pale and fluffy, at least 4 minutes. Beat in the {{eggs}} one at a time. Fold in the sifted {{plain-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Stir in the dissolved {{coffee-instant}} for the sponge, then fold in the {{walnuts}} for the sponge. Divide evenly between the two tins. Bake for 25–28 minutes until a skewer comes out clean and the sponges are pulling away from the edges.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} for the buttercream until pale. Add the sifted {{icing-sugar}} in three batches, beating between each addition. Beat in the dissolved {{coffee-instant}} for the buttercream until smooth and glossy. This is the
: it should taste distinctly of coffee.
Cool the sponges completely. Sandwich with one third of the buttercream. Spread the remaining buttercream over the top and sides. Press the walnut halves around the top edge to decorate.
